title:
OK, this is going to save a lot of time in Adobe Premiere! 🤩

You'll now find the new AI-powered Object Mask Tool in there, which works just like Photoshop's Object Selection tool.

text:
Rob de Winter
3mo
Edited
Report this post
OK, this is going to save a lot of time in
Adobe
Premiere! 🤩

You'll now find the new AI-powered Object Mask Tool in there, which works just like Photoshop's Object Selection tool. 

You can select anything in your video, and what's really impressive is how incredibly fast you can track the object afterwards, applying the mask across your entire video.

After that, you can use that mask on any effect in Premiere by dragging it to the effect you want. Think of Blur, Opacity, Color corrections and any other effect you can imagine.

What makes the new implementation even better: you can create your masks BEFORE applying any effects. Just create and track your mask (without an effect getting in the way of your view). Once your mask is ready and tracked, drag it onto any effect.

In the Object Mask tool group, you'll also find:
* The new Ellipse Mask Tool
* The Rectangle Mask Tool
* The Pen Mask Tool

That means they're no longer in the Effect Controls panel by default (update your muscle memory 😆). I think this works 100% better than the previous workflow.

The mask data gets saved in a separate folder next to your project file (called "Your Project Name Masks"). So when you share projects, make sure to include that folder.
